what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment that links a channel-memory entry to an image resource identified by a URI-like target.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
It declares an attachment of type 'images'.
standouts: The target uses a custom 'image://' URI scheme pointing to 'design-source/recent-pitches/p-35'.
It is a minimal, pointer-style ledger record rather than a content document.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
Provides id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
Acts as a lightweight attachment reference without embedded content.
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that points the design-source channel at a specific pitch image.
questions: What does 'p-35' represent and what is depicted in the image?
Who added this attachment and why?
Is the target image guaranteed to exist and be resolvable?
purpose_vs_actual: The fields collectively describe an attachment ledger record, and the content matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: Lightweight cross-references between records and assets
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to attach a recent pitch image to the design-source channel memory so the image can be located alongside related channel context.
novelty: The use of a custom 'image://' URI scheme as a target reference is a distinct way to address assets.
contribution: Provides a persistent, timestamped pointer that associates an image asset with a channel memory entry, enabling later retrieval of the attachment in context.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment ledger record in the design-source channel that references the image target image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-35 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To attach and index a pitch image in the design-source channel memory.
